title: Backtracking, Constraint Propagation, and Branch-and-Bound · Mastery Check
summary: Search schedules and assignments while proving why pruned branches cannot help. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a bounded combinatorial solver with pruning audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.
Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.
Use constrained-first and value-order heuristics without confusing speed with correctness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
